forecast-period.text: A strong high pressure system over the Tasman Sea will maintain a ridge across South Australia through to Friday. Northerly winds freshen on Friday ahead of a front that is forecast to move across the far west Friday afternoon, then continue across the state during the weekend. The low pressure system associated with this front is expected to linger over waters to the south of the state resulting in a trough moving over southern parts later Sunday and Monday. The low will then move into Bass Strait on Monday.
Wind and wave forecasts are averages. Wind gusts can be 40 per cent stronger than the forecast, and stronger still in squalls and thunderstorms. Maximum waves can be twice the forecast height.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones. Marine forecasts are also available on VHF and HF radio. View the radio schedule on www.bom.gov.au/marine
Check the latest Coastal Waters Forecasts for information on wind, wave and weather conditions for neighbouring coastal zones.

area.description: Far West Coast: WA-SA Border to Fowlers Bay

forecast-period.text: Northwest to northeasterly 10 to 15 knots, reaching up to 20 knots inshore in the late afternoon. Winds tending southeast to southwesterly in the evening.
Around 1 metre.
Southwesterly 1.5 to 2 metres.
Cloudy.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Friday for Far West Coast
forecast-period.text: Northwest to southwesterly 15 to 20 knots becoming southwesterly 15 to 25 knots in the early afternoon. Winds reaching up to 30 knots in the evening.
Below 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 2 metres during the afternoon.
Southwesterly 1.5 to 2 metres.
Cloudy. 95% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm.
Westerly 20 to 30 knots tending southwesterly 25 to 30 knots in the early afternoon then tending westerly 20 to 25 knots in the late afternoon.
1.5 to 2.5 metres.
Southwesterly 1.5 to 2 metres, increasing to 2 to 3 metres around midday.
Partly cloudy. 95% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the early morning.
Westerly 20 to 30 knots turning southwesterly 20 to 25 knots during the day.
2 metres.
Southwesterly 2 to 3 metres.
Partly cloudy. 95% chance of showers.

area.description: Upper West Coast: Fowlers Bay to Elliston

forecast-period.text: Northerly 10 to 15 knots turning northeasterly 15 to 20 knots in the late afternoon.
1 to 1.5 metres, decreasing below 1 metre by early evening.
Southwesterly 2 metres.
Mostly clear.
Northerly 15 to 25 knots turning westerly 10 to 15 knots in the early afternoon.
1 to 2 metres, decreasing to 1 metre during the afternoon.
Southwesterly 2 metres.
Cloudy. 90% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the late afternoon and evening.

Around 1 metre, increasing to 1.5 to 2.5 metres during the morning.
Southwesterly 2 metres, increasing to 2 to 3 metres around midday.
Partly cloudy. Near 100% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the early morning.
Westerly 20 to 30 knots.
1.5 to 2 metres.
Southwesterly 3 to 4 metres, decreasing to 2.5 to 3 metres during the morning.
Partly cloudy. 95% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the morning.

1 to 1.5 metres, decreasing to 1 metre by early evening.
Southwesterly 2 metres.

1 to 1.5 metres.

1 to 1.5 metres, increasing to 1.5 to 2 metres west of Kangaroo Island.
Southwesterly 2 to 2.5 metres.
Partly cloudy. 50% chance of showers west of Kangaroo Island, near zero chance elsewhere.
Northerly 15 to 25 knots turning west to northwesterly 20 to 30 knots during the morning then becoming northwesterly 20 to 25 knots in the early afternoon.
1.5 to 2.5 metres.
Southwesterly 2 metres, increasing to 2 to 3 metres during the morning.

1.5 to 2.5 metres.
